skill.name: libreoffice-impress
skill.description: Use when creating, editing, formatting, or extracting LibreOffice Impress (.odp) presentations via UNO, including slides, content placement, tables, charts, media, notes, master pages, find & replace, and snapshots.
skill.summary: Create and edit LibreOffice Impress presentations programmatically with Python
skill.author: dfk1352

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 91 high-severity patterns flagged as 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' MD5 markers and 70 'external_commands' patterns in SKILL.md documentation examples. After evaluation, all findings are false positives: MD5 markers are documentation formatting (SKILL.md:3,17-21,68), not cryptographic operations, and shell backticks are documentation examples showing proper CLI usage. The skill uses legitimate subprocess calls to LibreOffice with hardcoded arguments (scripts/uno_bridge.py:25,100, scripts/impress/snapshot.py:147) for document automation. Dynamic imports are for optional dependencies (content.py:7, core.py:5, slides.py:5, snapshot.py:9). Tempfile usage is properly scoped (snapshot.py:5,73). No evidence of malicious intent or security vulnerabilities.

high_findings.title: Misidentified MD5 Pattern in Documentation
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged MD5 as 'weak cryptographic algorithm' in SKILL.md. Evaluation shows these are NOT cryptographic operations - the 'MD5' strings appear in document filter names (line 14: 'Impress MS PowerPoint 2007 XML') and in example paths/format strings. No actual MD5 hashing is performed.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed false positive - SKILL.md contains only documentation and filter name references, no cryptographic code

high_findings.title: Misidentified Weak Crypto in Python Files
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged MD5/CLSID values as 'weak cryptographic algorithms' in Python source files. These are actually LibreOffice chart CLSID identifiers (charts.py:62,65,144,147) and filter constants (content.py:36-37,64-65,122-125,186-189,256-259), not cryptographic operations.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed false positive - these are LibreOffice API CLSID constants and filter names, not cryptographic code

high_findings.title: Dynamic Import Statements
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged dynamic import() as 'script' risk. These are legitimate lazy-loading patterns for optional UNO libraries that may not be installed until LibreOffice runtime is available.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Verified imports are for optional dependencies (uno, PIL) loaded after LibreOffice detection - legitimate pattern

high_findings.title: Subprocess Calls for LibreOffice
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged subprocess calls. These are legitimate invocations of the LibreOffice CLI (soffice) with hardcoded arguments for document automation - no user input injection risk.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Subprocess calls use hardcoded arguments for LibreOffice automation - no command injection vector exists

high_findings.title: Shell Backtick Examples in Documentation
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged backticks in SKILL.md as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. These are documentation examples showing CLI command syntax, not actual code execution in the skill.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed false positive - SKILL.md is documentation with example commands in backtick notation, not executable code

high_findings.title: Sensitive File References
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged certificate/key file references. These are actually file paths to user presentations and media files, not cryptographic keys or certificates.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Verified these are file path parameters for user documents (doc_path, image_path), not sensitive credential storage

high_findings.title: Temp File Operations
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged temp file creation. These are properly scoped temporary directory operations (tempfile.TemporaryDirectory context manager) used for slide snapshot export.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Proper use of tempfile.TemporaryDirectory with automatic cleanup - no security risk

content.value_statement: Automate LibreOffice Impress presentation creation, editing, and export without manual formatting. This skill provides Python functions to build slides, add content, insert charts and media, apply styling, and export to PDF or PNG.
actual_capabilities: Create new Impress presentations from scratch with custom slide layouts
Add and manipulate slides including title, content, text boxes, shapes, and images
Insert and configure charts (bar, line, pie, scatter) with custom data
Build and format tables with cell-level styling and data
Add audio and video media to slides
Apply master pages and custom backgrounds for consistent branding
Export presentations to PDF or PNG snapshot formats
Perform find and replace operations across entire presentations
limitations: Requires LibreOffice to be installed on the system
Cannot create animations, transitions, or interactive elements
Chart data population may fail silently for some chart types
Limited to ODP format for native editing; PowerPoint export has compatibility limits

use_cases.title: Generate Sales Deck from Data
use_cases.description: Automate quarterly sales presentations by populating slides with data from CSV files or databases. Create template decks with placeholders and fill them programmatically.
use_cases.target_user: Sales teams needing recurring report decks

use_cases.title: Batch Convert Presentation Archive
use_cases.description: Process large collections of presentations by applying consistent formatting, updating master pages, or converting all files to PDF for distribution.

use_cases.title: Build Training Materials
use_cases.description: Create consistent training slide decks by programmatically inserting media, charts, and standardized content blocks across multiple presentations.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic Presentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new Impress presentation at {output_path} with a title slide showing '{title}' and a content slide with bullet points: {bullets}
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new presentation from scratch

prompt_templates.title: Add Chart Slide
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a chart to slide {index} of {presentation_path}. Use chart type '{chart_type}' with this data: {data_table}. Set title to '{chart_title}'.

prompt_templates.title: Apply Brand Formatting
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply master page '{master_name}' to all slides in {presentation_path}. Then set the background color to '{background_color}'.
prompt_templates.scenario: Applying consistent branding across a deck

prompt_templates.title: Export for Distribution
prompt_templates.prompt: Export {presentation_path} to PDF at {output_path}. Also create PNG snapshots of slides {slide_indices} at {width}x{height} pixels.

output_examples.input: Create a new presentation with 3 slides: title slide with 'Q4 Review', a content slide with agenda items, and a chart slide with monthly sales data
output: Slide 2: Content slide with 4 agenda items
Slide 3: Bar chart with 3 months of sales data

output_examples.input: Apply the 'Corporate' master page and export all slides as PNG images

best_practices: Use absolute file paths for all document and media references to avoid path resolution issues
Set PYTHONPATH to include the skill scripts directory before importing impress modules
Use zero-based slide indices consistently throughout all operations
Verify slide content by using snapshot exports before finalizing presentations
anti_patterns: Using relative paths instead of absolute paths for document operations
Mixing one-based and zero-based slide indexing in the same operation
Passing lowercase layout names (use TITLE_SLIDE, not title_slide)
Attempting to modify slides before creating the presentation file

faq.question: Do I need LibreOffice installed to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill requires LibreOffice to be installed on the system. It uses the UNO API to control LibreOffice programmatically.

faq.question: Can this skill edit existing PowerPoint files?
faq.answer: The skill can load existing ODP files and export to PPTX, but some formatting may not translate perfectly. Test critical presentations.

faq.question: Why do chart updates sometimes fail silently?
faq.answer: LibreOffice chart API has limited support for real-time data updates. Use warnings to catch failures and verify chart content manually.

faq.question: How do I set up the Python path for this skill?
faq.answer: Set PYTHONPATH to include the scripts directory from this skill. For example: PYTHONPATH=/path/to/skills/libreoffice-impress/scripts

faq.question: Can I add animations or transitions with this skill?
faq.answer: No. The UNO API support for animations and transitions is limited. This skill focuses on static slide content.

faq.question: What image formats are supported for insertion?
faq.answer: LibreOffice supports common formats including PNG, JPG, GIF, and SVG. Use absolute paths for image files.
